Avanade appoints Doug Robinson as country CEO for Australia and New Zealand
items.summary: Avanade has appointed Doug Robinson as its country chief executive officer for Australia and New Zealand, effective 18 May 2026. Robinson, who is based in Australia, will lead Avanade’s business [...]

items.title: Using AI in your ERP? You should know where your data goes
items.summary: Finance teams need to know whether ERP AI queries cross borders, because model routing can affect sovereignty, compliance and audit trails.

items.title: Connected drone ecosystems reshape public safety ops
items.summary: Fragmented drone systems are slowing emergency decisions as agencies juggle multiple platforms, sensors and command tools in fast-moving incidents.

items.title: OpenText launches AI cyber tools for Australian firms
items.summary: Australian firms face rising cyber and compliance costs as OpenText adds tools to govern AI use, data access and application risks.

items.title: Dell launches PowerStore Elite for AI & cyber risk
items.summary: Enterprises under pressure from AI growth and ransomware get mixed-generation clustering, stronger recovery tools and higher capacity in Dell's new platform.

items.title: The AI challenge - balancing governance and innovation
items.summary: Many projects remain stuck at proof of concept as businesses wrestle with data governance, security and agent oversight before scaling AI.

items.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
items.summary: Verizon's "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" ("DBIR") finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

items.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
items.summary: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

items.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
items.summary: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

items.title: The Broken Physics of Remediation – exploitation now precedes disclosure
items.summary: We speak with Qualys Managing Director for ANZ, Sam Salehi in the lead up to their Customer and Channel Partner event in Sydney on 28 May, 2026. With what will [...]

items.title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
items.summary: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.

items.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
items.summary: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

items.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
items.summary: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

items.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
items.summary: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.

items.title: Researchers Wanted Preschool Teachers to Wear Cameras to Train AI
items.summary: “With your permission, your child’s lead teacher may wear a small teacher-worn camera that captures the teacher's approximate first-person perspective, and/or we may place a fixed video camera in the classroom,” a document given to parents and later shared with 404 Media reads.

items.title: The Boring Stuff Is Dangerous Now
items.summary: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

items.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
items.summary: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failures, and cringeworthy moments that still have us shaking our heads.

items.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
items.summary: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

items.title: Baidam and AUSCERT sign MOU to expand cybersecurity collaboration
items.summary: Baidam has signed a memorandum of understanding with AUSCERT to collaborate on cyber threat intelligence, incident response, phishing takedowns, security capability training and joint events. The agreement formalises a relationship [...]

items.title: Cowbell appoints Gerry Power as General Manager, Australia
items.summary: Cowbell has appointed Gerry Power as General Manager, Australia, as the cyber insurance provider expands its local operations. The newly created role will see Power lead market development, distribution strategy [...]

items.title: Aon appoints Quinton Kotze as head of cyber solutions in Australia
items.summary: Aon has appointed Quinton Kotze as head of cyber solutions in Australia, as organisations face increasing cyber risk and a tightening market for cyber insurance and related advisory services. Based [...]

items.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
items.summary: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

items.title: Equinix expands Fabric Geo Zones to Australia
items.summary: Equinix has expanded its Equinix Fabric “Geo Zones” capability to Australia as part of a broader rollout across five continents, positioning the feature as a way for organisations to enforce [...]

items.title: Rapid7 analysis flags Cisco and Palo Alto authentication bypass vulnerabilities
items.summary: Rapid7 has published analysis of two newly disclosed authentication bypass vulnerabilities affecting enterprise networking and security platforms from Cisco and Palo Alto Networks, with both vendors urging organisations to patch [...]

items.title: Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
items.summary: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

items.title: Maximum Severity Cisco SD-WAN Bug Exploited in the Wild
items.summary: This is the second time this year a threat actor has leveraged a CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Cisco's network control system.

items.title: DOGE Cuts Unleashed a Deadly Wave of Violence Across Africa, Study Finds
items.summary: The dismantling of the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) is associated with measurable increases in Africa, especially in areas most dependent on the agency’s support.

items.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
items.summary: Attackers uniquely fingerprint victims before delivering spear-phishing payloads aimed at espionage, in the latest campaign from the Belarussian nation-state threat group.

items.title: Foxconn Attack Highlights Manufacturing's Cyber Crisis
items.summary: A Nitrogen ransomware attack on Foxconn's North American facilities is one of 600 hits on manufacturers this year, as gangs increasingly target the sector for its low tolerance for downtime.

items.title: Welcoming the Bahamian Government to Have I Been Pwned
items.summary: Today, we welcome the 44th government onboarded to Have I Been Pwned’s free gov service: The Bahamas. items.title: Study Finds 1 in 10 Australian Organisations Could Lose Control After AI Credential Exposure
items.summary: Australian organisations are rapidly adopting AI agents for sensitive security tasks, but a new study suggests many may lack the governance and recovery capability to manage the risks if those [...]

items.title: Checkbox Assessments Aren't Fit to Measure Risk
items.summary: Security governance needs to be more than an annual compliance exercise. New companies are emerging to address risk-management gaps in current audit tools.

items.title: Attackers Weaponize RubyGems for Data Dead Drops
items.summary: Threat actors are publishing RubyGems packages that include scrapers targeting public-facing UK government servers, but with no clear objective.

items.title: Tables Turn on 'The Gentlemen' RaaS Gang With Data Leak
items.summary: An OPSEC failure provides a window into what helped the ransomware group rise: a generous affiliate model, opportunistic TTPs, and an effective organizational structure.

items.title: Podcast: The Chinese Deepfake Software Powering Scams
items.summary: We got Haotian AI, the Chinese-language deepfake software powering scams. We also talk about a man finding $1 million of Yu-Gi-Oh cards, and how the AI hard drive shortage is impacting internet archiving.

items.title: LatAm Vibe Hackers Generate Custom Hacking Tools on the Fly
items.summary: In the latest evolution of automated cyberattacks, threat actors heavily leveraged AI agents to support campaigns against entities in Mexico and Brazil.

items.summary: Cyber security executives have raised concerns that the Federal Budget’s digital and AI initiatives may not be matched by sufficient investment in cyber resilience, as government and critical infrastructure face [...]

items.title: Infosys opens security operations centre in North Sydney to expand ANZ cyber services
items.summary: Infosys has launched its first dedicated Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) in Australia, based at its North Sydney office, as it expands cybersecurity service delivery across Australia and New Zealand. [...]

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.summary: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- in

items.title: It's Patch Tuesday for Microsoft & Not a Zero-Day In Sight
items.summary: It's the first time in two years with no zero-days. But with 137 flaws to patch, including nine critical ones, admins still have plenty of work to do.

items.title: ISACA poll finds AI adoption outpaces governance and ROI
items.summary: A global ISACA survey of more than 3,400 digital trust professionals has found most organisations believe staff are already using artificial intelligence, but many report weak governance, limited visibility of [...]

items.title: 'Dirty Frag' Exploit Poised to Blow Up on Enterprise Linux Distros
items.summary: The privilege escalation vulnerability, which is similar to other Linux flaws like Copy Fail and Dirty Pipe, may already be under limited exploitation.

items.title: Hackers Use AI for Exploit Development, Attack Automation
items.summary: Cyber adversaries have long used AI, but now attackers are using large language models to develop exploits and orchestrate complex attacks.

items.title: Cyber Espionage Group Targets Aviation Firms to Steal Map Data
items.summary: The campaign quietly compromises aerospace and drone operators to exfiltrate GIS files, terrain models, and GPS data and gain a clear picture of adversaries' world view.

items.summary: Fastly has appointed Bruce Bennie as Area Vice President, Sales for Australia and New Zealand, based in Sydney. The company said Bennie will oversee Fastly’s business across the region, focusing [...]

items.title: ShinyHunters Claims Second Attack Against Instructure
items.summary: The edtech company is struggling to wrest control from its hackers. PII belonging to hundreds of millions of people is on the line.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide

items.title: After Replacing TeamPCP Malware, 'PCPJack' Steals Cloud Secrets
items.summary: PCPJack makes innovative use of parquet files for stealthy, pre-validated target discovery as it canvasses multiple cloud environments.

items.title: 'TrustFall' Convention Exposes Claude Code Execution Risk
items.summary: Malicious repositories can trigger code execution in Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, and CoPilot CLI with minimal or no user interaction, thanks to skimpy warning dialogs.

items.summary: A third-party data breach disclosed by the Queensland Department of Education has renewed scrutiny on cyber risks tied to vendors and online learning platforms used across the education sector. In [...]

items.title: VoidStealer Malware Darts Past Google Chrome's Encryption
items.summary: Authors of the VoidStealer Trojan uncovered yet another way to get around Google's App-Bound Encryption (ABE), opening the door to infostealers.

items.title: Instructure Breach Exposes Schools' Vendor Dependence
items.summary: ShinyHunters' attack on Instructure, which owns the widely used Canvas learning management system (LMS), carries big questions about the trust educational institutions put into their vendors.

items.title: From Stuxnet to ChatGPT: 20 News Events That Shaped Cyber
items.summary: As part of its 20th anniversary celebration, Dark Reading looks back on 20 of the biggest newsmaking events from the past two decades that influenced the risk landscape for today's cybersecurity teams.

items.title: Middle East Cyber Battle Field Broadens — Especially in UAE
items.summary: As the war with Iran continues, breach attempts targeting the United Arab Emirates tripled in a few weeks — many targeting critical infrastructure.
